summary: Frontend-only regression test of the new enterprise mega-menu navigation on clouDroplets.IO. Verified all desktop mega panels (corporate/solutions/services/technologies/industries/resources), hover open, mouse-into-panel-keeps-open, click-to-scroll, Escape closes, scroll closes, Terms route navigation, mobile hamburger + accordion, and regression on Services accordion. 31/31 checks passed.

issues: Floating WhatsApp button (bottom-left) overlaps the 'Get in Touch' CTA inside the open mobile menu at 390x844, partially covering the CTA label. Minor priority.

action_items: Optional: In mobile menu open state, hide or lower z-index of the WhatsApp floating button so it does not overlap the 'Get in Touch' CTA.
Minor console warning from Lenis: 'container has a non-static position...' — non-blocking; consider adding position:relative to the scroll container if you want a clean console.
critical_code_review_comments: Header.jsx (341 lines) is fine size-wise; MENUS could be extracted to a config module for reuse.
Multiple mega-item elements share the same data-testid=mega-item-<menuId>; acceptable since tests use nth-selection, but consider unique per-item testids for finer-grained testing.
goTo relies on window.__lenis; if Lenis fails to initialise, smooth-scroll silently no-ops (still works during test since Lenis loaded).
context_for_next_testing_agent: New nav testids: nav-<id>, nav-contact, mega-<id>, mega-item-<id>, mobile-menu-toggle, mobile-menu, mobile-nav-<id>, logo-home, header-cta, site-header. Old testids (nav-manifesto etc) removed. Desktop nav appears at >=1024px only; below that hamburger is shown. window.__lenis is exposed for smooth scroll.
